items.q: In Milgram's study, what percentage of participants gave the maximum 450V shock?
items.why: Students remember 'most' obeyed and assume all did. Actually 65% went to 450V; 35% defied. This nuance matters because it shows obedience is strong but not absolute.
Students reverse IV and DV. The IV is what you CHANGE (input); the DV is what you MEASURE (outcome). Mnemonic: 'I change the I.'

items.q: According to Maslow, when can self-actualisation be achieved?
items.correct: Only after lower deficiency needs are largely met.
items.misconception: Anyone can self-actualise at any time through willpower.
items.why: Students miss the hierarchical structure. You cannot pursue growth needs while physiological, safety, love, and esteem needs are unmet - that's the whole point of a hierarchy.
The answer examiners want is: Steele and Aronson. “Tajfel and Turner” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: The individualism-collectivism cultural dimension was identified by:
items.why: Correct answer: Hofstede. “Asch” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Tajfel and Turner publish social identity theory?
items.why: Exact answer: 1979 AD. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
